scope: Context Menu and UI Drag Event Handling
interaction_flow: Caller → UIContextMenu: UIContextMenu({ items, id, position, delay, parent_id, is_submenu, css, parent_element, onClose })
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: build HTML string (context-menu div + <li> items/submenus/dividers)
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: compute x_pos/y_pos from (mouseX/mouseY or touch or options.position) vs window bounds
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: $(contextMenu).css({ top, left }); apply options.css if present
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: show/fadeIn based on options.delay (false=show(0), true/1/undefined=fadeIn(50), number=fadeIn(n))
UIContextMenu → menuAim: $(contextMenu).menuAim({ rowSelector, submenuSelector, submenuDirection, enter, activate, deactivate, exit })
UIContextMenu → UIContextMenu: fade_remove(this) if not submenu item
jQuery/DOM → menuAim: mousemoveDocument — push {x,y} to mouseLocs (max 3)
jQuery/DOM → menuAim: mouseenterRow — clearTimeout(timeoutId), options.enter(row), possiblyActivate(row)
menuAim → menuAim: activationDelay — computes slope from mouseLocs vs submenu corners; returns DELAY(300ms) or 0
menuAim → menuAim: [if delay>0] setTimeout(possiblyActivate, delay) ELSE activate(row)
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: remove other open submenus $('.context-menu[data-parent-id]')
UIContextMenu → UIContextMenu: [if has-submenu] setTimeout(300ms) → UIContextMenu({ items: subItems, parent_id, is_submenu:true, id, position })
jQuery/DOM → menuAim: mouseleaveRow — if not has-open-submenu, remove active class; options.exit(row)
menuAim → UIContextMenu: options.deactivate(row) [if row was activeRow]
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: remove submenu if row had open submenu, removeClass('has-open-context-menu-submenu')
jQuery/DOM → UIContextMenu: remove 'context-menu-item-active' from all items; clearTimeout(submenu_delay_timer)
jQuery/DOM → menuAim: mouseleaveMenu — clearTimeout(timeoutId); options.exitMenu() → options.deactivate(activeRow) if true
UIContextMenu → jQuery/DOM: remove() or fadeOut(200, remove) on contextMenu + parent menu
jQuery/DOM → UIContextMenu: 'remove' event fires → clearTimeout, options.onClose(cancel_options_), restore parent scroll/overflow
taphold.setup(data) → jQuery/DOM: bind touchstart/mousedown → startHandler; touchend/mouseup → stopHandler; touchmove/mouseleave → leaveHandler
startHandler → $elem: store click handler, reset flags (taphold_triggered, taphold_clicked, taphold_cancelled=false)
startHandler → $elem: set taphold_timer = setTimeout(duration) → if not cancelled/clicked: $elem.trigger('taphold'), taphold_triggered=true
stopHandler → $elem: if taphold_cancelled → return; clearTimeout(taphold_timer); if not triggered/clicked → restore click handler, trigger click, taphold_clicked=true
leaveHandler → $elem: set taphold_cancelled = true (cancels pending hold)
taphold.teardown() → jQuery/DOM: unbind startHandler, stopHandler, leaveHandler
Caller → set_menu_item_prop: window.set_menu_item_prop(items, item_id, prop, val)
set_menu_item_prop → set_menu_item_prop: iterate items; if item.id === item_id → item[prop]=val, break; else if item.items → recurse
key_relationships: UIContextMenu creates and owns menuAim ($.fn.menuAim initialized on contextMenu DOM element)
UIContextMenu recursively calls UIContextMenu for nested submenus (is_submenu:true)
menuAim callbacks (activate, deactivate, exit, enter, exitMenu) are closures inside UIContextMenu — full access to menu_id, contextMenu, submenu_delay_timer
taphold is a self-contained jQuery special event plugin — no direct coupling to UIContextMenu
set_menu_item_prop is a standalone window utility — operates on items array passed by caller

key_data_structures.options.items[i]: { html, html_active?, icon?, icon_active?, disabled?, checked?, onClick?(event), action?(), val?, id?, items?[] }

important_notes: menuAim uses mouse trajectory slope analysis (not just timeouts) to avoid premature submenu switches
Submenu open is delayed 300ms after activate; deactivate cancels that timer
UIContextMenu disables pointer-events on active iframe at startup: $('.window-active .window-app-iframe').css('pointer-events','none')
submenuDirection() is a function returning 'right'/'left' based on available window space
Menu positioning checks both right-overflow (subtract menu_width) and bottom-overflow (clamp to taskbar top)
Keyboard navigation supported: data?.keyboard flag bypasses mouse-position equality check in activate
Parent element gets overflow:hidden and class 'children-have-open-contextmenu' while menu is open; restored on remove
taphold duration defaults to 500ms; supports custom clickHandler option
